title: Automated Client Reporting for E-Commerce
metaDescription: Done-for-you recurring client reports for e-commerce. Sales, ad spend, and inventory pulled on schedule and delivered in your voice.
excerpt: A Cadence layer that pulls e-commerce numbers on schedule and ships clean client reports without you opening a spreadsheet or chasing data.

sections.intro:
If you run or serve an online retail business, reporting is the work that always slips. Sales, ad spend, returns, inventory, and channel performance live in five different places, and somebody has to stitch them together every week or every month. That somebody is usually you, late on a Sunday, copying numbers into a deck while the store keeps trading.

The AIOS fixes this by treating reporting as a scheduled job, not a task. Your AI Operating System knows your store, knows your KPIs, pulls the numbers from the tools you already use, drafts the report in your voice, and delivers it on the day and time you choose. You review, you approve, you ship. The owner still owns the system.
sections.fourCFit:
This playbook sits in the Cadence layer of the Four Cs. Cadence is the part of the AIOS that runs on a schedule without being asked, so reporting happens whether you remember or not.

  • Context: your offers, clients, store structure, KPIs, and voice are loaded once so reports sound like you wrote them.
  • Connections: the system reads from your store platform, ad accounts, email or SMS tool, and analytics, working from live data.
  • Capabilities: drafting, summarizing, flagging anomalies, and comparing periods.
  • Cadence: the schedule itself. Weekly, biweekly, or monthly, on your chosen day and time, every time.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
You get a recurring client reporting workflow that produces a real artifact, on a real schedule, with a real SLA.

  • A branded report (PDF, doc, or shared link) covering revenue, orders, AOV, conversion rate, ad spend and ROAS by channel, top SKUs, returns, and inventory flags.
  • A short written summary at the top in your voice: what moved, why it likely moved, and what to do next.
  • Period comparisons (week over week, month over month, year over year) without manual pivots.
  • Delivery on a fixed cadence: weekly by Monday 9am, monthly by the 3rd, or whatever you choose.
  • SLA: report drafted and queued for your review at least 12 hours before client delivery time. If something is missing, you get an alert, not a silent failure.
  • One named owner of the workflow on our side, and a versioned spec so you always know what is being measured.
sections.howWeInstall:
Installation runs in three phases, the same for every AIOS workflow.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We map your current reporting, the tools holding the data, your client list, and where time is being lost. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We connect your data sources, build the report template, load your voice and KPIs, set the schedule, and run two dry deliveries against real periods so you can sign off before any client sees it.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The Cadence runs on its own. We monitor, fix breakages, and ship one new automation per week as your needs grow. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Reporting time per client drops from 2 to 5 hours per cycle to about 15 minutes of review.
  • Reports go out on time, every time, instead of slipping by a few days at the end of the month.
  • Client churn tied to "I never hear from them" tends to fall, because the cadence itself is the communication.
  • For agencies serving 10 or more e-commerce clients, this layer alone typically returns 20 to 40 hours per month.
  • Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Do you replace our existing reporting tool?

No. We work with what you already use for analytics, ads, and your store. The AIOS sits on top and handles the assembly, narrative, and delivery on a schedule.

What if a data source breaks or an API changes?

The Cadence layer is monitored. If a source fails, you get an alert and we fix the connection. You will never send a client a report full of zeroes without knowing first.

Can each client get a different report?

Yes. Templates are per client or per segment. One client can get a one-page weekly summary, another can get a full monthly deck, all from the same system.
